channel.title: Blog on TDJ28
channel.description: Recent content in Blog on TDJ28

item.title: Wiggle while you work: using jittering to augment object tracking in low fps videos
item.description: Dealing with low fps video can make re-identification of individuals between frames more difficult as the potential targets tend to “jump” between frames in a way that throws off trackers. Here, I show how applying a Gaussian jitter or wiggle to artificially increase the fps of the video can improve tracking.

item.title: Quantum Computers: Mathieu's Equations and the Quadrupole Ion Traps
item.description: Of great use to physics, including one of the promising paths towards quantum computers, is the ion trap. Though there are various models in use, we focus here on Paul style models. To do so, we need understand Mathieu’s equations and their corresponding solutions in enough detail to grasp the function of the Paul ion trap. In this report, we explore the behavior of Mathieu’s equation and their solution for the Paul ion trap.

item.title: Lil' LLM: Part 1
item.description: In this first part in a multipart series, we build a little model LLM that can construct basic phrases and display the effects of temperature. This first version doesn’t use neural networks but does demonstrate some fundamentals of LLM terminology.

item.title: Bayesian look at rapid tests
item.description: A look at how Bayesian statistics can help us understand ways to make decisions (in the context of COVID-19 rapid test accuracy). This post will introduce concepts related to Bayesian statistics, but may still be interesting to individuals already familiar with the topic.

item.title: Using YOLO people detection with Segment Anything 2 from META
item.description: A quick and dirty example of creating a chef cookbook with rspec tests.

item.title: Automating the circularization of Apollo in Kerbal with python, KRPC, and Physics
item.description: Using the krpc library, we can automate a rocket simulator, turnking Kerbal space program into a more powerful educational tool. In this post, we automate the launching and circularization of the Kerbal equivalent of the Apollo rocket.

item.title: Rössler Strange Attractor with Python: Part 1
Headless Python Selenium With Behave
item.description: Selenium Testing a Web App can be a quick and productive win.

item.title: Using PNNL's new Cividis colormap in data science to make accessible heat maps
item.description: Recent work at PNNL has introduced a mathematically optimized colormap called Cividis that optimizes viewing of heatmaps and other plots for both those with and without full color vision

item.title: Exploring Wikipedia Page Count Trends
item.description: There are some readily available tools in python for doing time series analysis, and these tools can be used to study Wikipedia page count stats.

item.title: A Risk Analysis Bowtie Diagram in Latex
item.description: Couldn’t find a good example on the web, wrote one instead.

item.title: TRAPPIST-1 is probably not alive
item.description: While the findings of additional potentially habitable planets around TRAPPIST-1 is exciting, it also isn’t overly promising.

item.title: Putting a Space Station Over Philly With Final Cut Pro
item.description: A quick and easy (though not free) way to put a imaginary space station over Philadelphia.

item.title: DevOps 101: Chef Cookbook with Testing
item.description: A quick and dirty example of creating a chef cookbook with rspec tests.

item.title: Python Uses Operational Chaining for Boolean Comparisons
item.description: It is important to understand Python’s use of operational chaining to avoid programming errors.

item.title: Abstracting Networking with Docker Containers
item.description: Docker comes with a default network, but you can make your own network of unlimited complexity.
